executiveSummary.text: Comcast trades at 5.5x earnings with a 3.2% dividend yield — the market is pricing a structural and permanent earnings decline. That may be too pessimistic. Q1 2026 earnings on April 23 are expected to show a sharp EPS decline (consensus $0.82 vs $1.08 in Q1 2025, a fall of approximately 24%), but the Xfinity StreamSaver bundles launched in April 2026 — now incorporating Peacock alongside Netflix, Apple TV, Disney+, Hulu, and HBO Max in a single marketplace — represent a meaningful strategic pivot. If cord-cutting headwinds are priced in too aggressively, Comcast is a deep-value case. SPECULATIVE BUY reflects the compelling valuation support at 5.5x but not a high-conviction view given the secular cable subscriber losses that remain the primary bear argument.
